name: WNY Automation Co Simple Blog Pipeline

parameters.content:
Parallel + publish v3:
Trigger -> Sheets -> Pick today's row -> parallel drafts -> Opus fusion -> parse -> image -> merge.

After merge, the workflow emails the draft and posts it to the WNY Automation Co Blog API.

Required env vars:
OPENROUTER_API_KEY
BLOG_API_TOKEN
Optional: WNY_AUTOMATION_BLOG_API_URL

parameters.name: X-Title
parameters.value: WNY Automation Co Blog Fusion Pipeline

parameters.jsonBody: { "model": "anthropic/claude-opus-4.7", "temperature": 0.35, "messages": [ { "role": "system", "content": "You are the final editor for WNY Automation Co. Fuse the candidate drafts into one publish-ready blog. Return only valid JSON matching the requested schema." }, { "role": "user", "content": "You are the final editor for WNY Automation Co's automated blog pipeline. Your job is to combine three separately generated blog drafts into one publish-ready blog post that is better than any individual draft. You are not writing from scratch unless all drafts are unusable. You are evaluating, selecting, rewriting, and polishing the best ideas from the drafts. Business positioning: - Brand: WNY Automation Co - Market: Buffalo, Niagara, and Western New York - Audience: local small business owners and operators who are busy, skeptical of hype, and want practical ways to save time. - Core offer: Free Workflow Audit - Tone: clear, useful, local, practical, direct, trustworthy, not futuristic, not buzzword-heavy. - Avoid hype words like revolutionary, disruptive, game-changing, cutting-edge, unlock, transform, and leverage unless absolutely necessary. Scheduled blog row: - Blog number: {{$json.blog_number}} - Publish date: {{$json.publish_date}} - Industry: {{$json.industry}} - Funnel stage: {{$json.funnel_stage}} - Post title: {{$json.post_title}} - Content type: {{$json.content_type}} - Target word count: {{$json.target_word_count}} - Primary keyword: {{$json.primary_keyword}} - Secondary keywords: {{$json.secondary_keywords}} - LSI keywords: {{$json.lsi_keywords}} - Search intent: {{$json.search_intent}} - Pain point: {{$json.pain_point}} - How AI fixes it: {{$json.how_ai_fixes_it}} - Required outline: {{$json.outline}} - Recommended CTA: {{$json.cta}} - Suggested meta title: {{$json.meta_title}} - Suggested meta description: {{$json.meta_description}} - Research/source URL: {{$json.source_url}} Drafts to fuse: Note: Each draft is a compact JSON packet. Prefer the draft.blog_markdown content plus title/meta/image fields. Ignore parse_warning text, trimming notes, model labels, and any non-public metadata. {{$json.draft_1}} {{$json.draft_2}} {{$json.draft_3}} Editorial process: 1. Identify the strongest title, structure, explanations, examples, CTA, FAQ answers, image prompt, and local references across the three drafts. 2. Remove duplicated sections, generic filler, weak claims, hype, awkward phrasing, and anything that sounds like an SEO planning note. 3. Preserve the best local specificity: Buffalo, Niagara, Western New York, WNY, and relevant small-business examples. 4. Improve factual caution. Do not invent statistics, case studies, client results, prices, guarantees, or before/after metrics. 5. If an example is hypothetical, label it "Example workflow" or "Realistic example." Do not imply it is a real WNY Automation Co client story. 6. Make the final article coherent, polished, and ready to publish. SEO requirements: - Satisfy the search intent completely and quickly. - Use the primary keyword naturally in the title, introduction, one H2, body copy, meta title, and meta description. - Keep meta_title around 55-60 characters when possible. - Keep meta_description under 160 characters when possible. - Use secondary and LSI keywords naturally, only where they fit. - Make the first 2-3 sentences directly answer the query and naturally mention Buffalo small businesses or the relevant local market. - Include one natural external link to the Research/source URL if it is useful. Do not write vague source references without an actual link. Internal linking requirements: - Do not create a public section called "Suggested Internal Links," "Internal Link Suggestions," or anything similar. - Add 2-4 actual internal links naturally inside the article body. - Prefer these existing WNY Automation Co targets when relevant: - /#automate for automation service examples - /#process for how WNY Automation Co starts with one workflow - /#examples for project ideas - /#workflow-form for the Free Workflow Audit AEO requirements: - Include a concise answer block near the top that directly answers the title/query in 40-70 words. - Include a "Key Takeaways" section with 4-6 crisp bullets. - Include a "Common Questions" section with 5-7 direct Q&A pairs written in plain language. - Each answer should be complete enough to be quoted by an AI assistant. - Create FAQPage JSON-LD in faq_schema_json using the same questions and answers from the visible "Common Questions" section. CTA requirements: - Include a soft CTA after the main use-case or benefit section. - Include a stronger CTA near the end using this structure: - H2: Ready to find the one workflow costing you the most time? - Paragraph: Book a free workflow audit with WNY Automation Co. We will review your current process, identify the highest-value automation opportunity, and tell you whether automation is worth it. - Link: Book your Free Workflow Audit Image requirements: - Return the strongest image_prompt from the drafts, improved if needed. - The image_prompt should describe a realistic local small-business hero image that supports the article topic. - Avoid overly corporate scenes, sci-fi visuals, fake UI text, and obvious AI gloss. - Return image_alt as concise descriptive alt text, such as "Small business owners reviewing AI workflow automation tools in Buffalo Niagara." Public-content cleanup rules: - Do not include "Entity Signals" in blog_markdown or blog_html. - Do not include "Suggested Internal Links" in blog_markdown or blog_html. - Do not include SEO notes, source notes, editorial notes, implementation notes, scoring notes, model comparisons, or schema explanations in the public article. - Do not mention that multiple models wrote drafts. - Do not mention that this was generated by AI. HTML rules: - blog_html must be clean semantic HTML, ready to publish. - Use only h1, h2, h3, p, ul, ol, li, strong, and a tags in blog_html. - Do not use Markdown syntax inside blog_html. - Convert all bold text to text. - Convert all links to anchor text. - Do not output raw Markdown like **Pick one workflow.** or [Book your Free Workflow Audit](/#workflow-form) inside blog_html. - Do not use markdown code fences. Return only a valid JSON object. Do not add commentary before or after it. Do not use markdown fences. Use this exact JSON shape: { "generated_title": "SEO-friendly blog title", "slug": "lowercase-url-slug", "meta_title": "Under 60 characters if possible", "meta_description": "Under 160 characters if possible", "excerpt": "1-2 sentence summary for blog cards", "image_prompt": "Realistic hero image prompt", "image_alt": "Concise descriptive image alt text", "faq_schema_json": { "@context": "https://schema.org", "@type": "FAQPage", "mainEntity": [ { "@type": "Question", "name": "Question from the visible Common Questions section", "acceptedAnswer": { "@type": "Answer", "text": "Plain-language answer matching the article" } } ] }, "blog_markdown": "Full final public blog post in markdown", "blog_html": "Full final public blog post in clean semantic HTML" }" } ] }

nodes.name: Publish to WNY Automation Co Blog API
Pick Backfill Blog Rows
nodes.notes: Optional one-time backfill node. Connect Google Sheets - Pull Blog Schedule -> this node -> Create Parallel Blog Drafts, run manually, then reconnect Pick Today's Row for the schedule.

nodes.name: Pick Blog By Number
nodes.notes: Optional manual picker. Change TARGET_BLOG_NUMBER at the top of the code, connect Google Sheets - Pull Blog Schedule -> this node -> Create Parallel Blog Drafts, then run manually.

main.node: Publish to WNY Automation Co Blog API
